title: Ganesh Chalisa
intro: The Ganesh Chalisa is a powerful devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Ganesha, the remover of obstacles and the god of new beginnings.
description:
Shri Ganesh Chalisa

॥ Doha ॥
Jay Ganpati Sadgun Sadan, Kavivar Badan Kripal.
Vighna Haran Mangal Karan, Jay Jay Girijalal.

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jay Jay Jay Ganpati Ganraju.
Mangal Bharan Karan Shubh Kaju.
Jay Gajbadan Sadan Sukhdata.
Vishwa Vinayaka Buddhi Vidhata.
Vakra Tund Shuchi Shund Suhavan.
Tilak Tripund Bhal Man Bhavan.
Rajit Mani Muktan Ur Mala.
Swarn Mukut Shir Nayan Vishala.
Pustak Pani Kuthar Trishulam.
Modak Bhog Sugandhit Phulam.
Sundar Pitambar Tan Sajit.
Charan Paduka Muni Man Rajit.
Dhani Shivsuvan Shadanan Bhrata.
Gauri Lalan Vishwa-Vikhyata.
Riddhi-Siddhi Tav Chanwar Sudhare.
Mushak Vahan Sohat Dware.
Kahau Janm Shubh Katha Tumhari.
Ati Shuchi Pavan Mangal Kari.
Ek Samay Giriraj Kumari.
Putra Hetu Tap Kinha Bhari.
Bhayo Yagya Jab Purna Anupa.
Tab Pahunchyo Tum Dhari Dwij Rupa.
Atithi Jani Kai Gauri Sukhari.
Bahu Vidhi Seva Kari Tumhari.
Ati Prasann Hvai Tum Var Dinha.
Matu Putra Hit Jo Tap Kinha.
Milahi Putra Tuhi, Buddhi Vishala.
Bina Garbh Dharan Yahi Kala.
Ganayak Gun Gyan Nidhana.
Pujit Pratham Rup Bhagwana.
As Kahi Antardhyan Rup Hvai.
Palna Par Balak Swarup Hvai.
Bani Shishu Rudan Jabahin Tum Thana.
Lakhi Mukh Sukh Nahin Gauri Samana.
Sakal Magan, Sukhmangal Gavahin.
Nabh Te Suran Suman Varshavahin.
Shambhu, Uma, Bahu Daan Lutavahin.
Sur Munijan, Sut Dekhan Aavahin.
Lakhi Ati Anand Mangal Saja.
Dekhan Bhi Aaye Shani Raja.
Nij Avgun Guni Shani Man Mahi.
Balak, Dekhan Chahat Nahi.
Girija Kachhu Man Bhed Badhayo.
Utsav Mor, Na Shani Tuhi Bhayo.
Kahan Lage Shani, Man Sakuchai.
Ka Karihau, Shishu Mohi Dikhai.
Nahin Vishwas Uma Ur Bhayau.
Shani So Balak Dekhan Kahayau.
Padtahti Shani Drig Kon Prakasha.
Balak Sir Udi Gayo Akasha.
Girija Giri Vikal Hvai Dharani.
So Duhkh Dasha Gayo Nahin Varani.
Hahakar Machyau Kailasha.
Shani Kinhon Lakhi Sut Ko Nasha.
Turat Garuda Chadhi Vishnu Sidhaye.
Kati Chakra Son Gaj Shir Laye.
Balak Ke Dhad Upar Dharayo.
Pran Mantra Padhi Shankar Darayo.
Naam 'Ganesh' Shambhu Tab Kinhe.
Pratham Pujya Buddhi Nidhi Var Dinhe.
Buddhi Pariksha Jab Shiv Kinha.
Prithvi Kar Pradakshina Linha.
Chale Shadanan Bharami Bhulai.
Rache Baith Tum Buddhi Upai.
Charan Matu-Pitu Ke Dhar Linhen.
Tinke Saat Pradakshina Kinhen.
Dhani Ganesh Kahi Shiv Hiya Harshe.
Nabh Te Suran Suman Bahu Barse.
Tumhari Mahima Buddhi Badai.
Shesh Sahas Mukh Sake Na Gai.
Main Mati Hin Malin Dukhari.
Karahun Kaun Vidhi Vinay Tumhari.
Bhajat 'Ramsundar' Prabhudasa.
Jag Prayag, Kakara Durvasa.
Ab Prabhu Daya Din Par Kijai.
Apni Bhakti Shakti Kuch Dijai.

॥ Doha ॥
Shri Ganesh Yeh Chalisa, Path Karai Dhari Dhyan.
Nit Nav Mangal Mod Lahi, Milai Jagat Sanman.
Sambandh Apna Sahasra Dash, Rishi Panchami Dinesh.
Puran Chalisa Bhayo, Mangal Murti Ganesh.
meaning:
Shri Ganesh Chalisa (With English Meaning)

॥ Doha ॥
Jay Ganpati Sadgun Sadan, Kavivar Badan Kripal.
Victory to the abode of virtues, Ganpati, the kind-faced Lord.
Vighna Haran Mangal Karan, Jay Jay Girijalal.
Victory to the remover of obstacles and the cause of auspiciousness, the son of Girija.

॥ Chaupai ॥
Jay Jay Jay Ganpati Ganraju.
Victory, victory, victory to Ganpati, the King of Ganas.
Mangal Bharan Karan Shubh Kaju.
You are the abode of auspiciousness and the fulfiller of all good works.
Jay Gajbadan Sadan Sukhdata.
Victory to the elephant-faced one, the giver of happiness.
Vishwa Vinayaka Buddhi Vidhata.
Victory to the Lord of the world, Vinayaka, the bestower of wisdom.
Vakra Tund Shuchi Shund Suhavan.
Your curved trunk is pure and beautiful.
Tilak Tripund Bhal Man Bhavan.
The tripund (three-lined) mark on your forehead is pleasing to the mind.
Rajit Mani Muktan Ur Mala.
A necklace of gems and pearls adorns your chest.
Swarn Mukut Shir Nayan Vishala.
You wear a golden crown on your head and have wide, divine eyes.
Pustak Pani Kuthar Trishulam.
You hold a book, an axe, and a trident in your hands.
Modak Bhog Sugandhit Phulam.
You love the offering of Modaks and fragrant flowers.
Sundar Pitambar Tan Sajit.
You are adorned with beautiful yellow garments.
Charan Paduka Muni Man Rajit.
Your wooden sandals reside in the hearts of the sages.
Dhani Shivsuvan Shadanan Bhrata.
Blessed are you, the son of Shiva and brother of Shadanan (Kartikeya).
Gauri Lalan Vishwa-Vikhyata.
O son of Gauri (Parvati), you are world-renowned.
Riddhi-Siddhi Tav Chanwar Sudhare.
Riddhi and Siddhi (your wives) stand by to fan you with whisks.
Mushak Vahan Sohat Dware.
Your vehicle, the mouse, looks magnificent at your door.
Kahau Janm Shubh Katha Tumhari.
I narrate your auspicious birth story.
Ati Shuchi Pavan Mangal Kari.
It is extremely pure, holy, and brings prosperity.
Ek Samay Giriraj Kumari.
Once, the daughter of the mountain (Parvati).
Putra Hetu Tap Kinha Bhari.
Performed great penance to have a son.
Bhayo Yagya Jab Purna Anupa.
When the incomparable sacrifice (Yagya) was completed.
Tab Pahunchyo Tum Dhari Dwij Rupa.
You arrived there in the form of a Brahmin.
Atithi Jani Kai Gauri Sukhari.
Recognizing you as a guest, Gauri was filled with joy.
Bahu Vidhi Seva Kari Tumhari.
She served you in many ways.
Ati Prasann Hvai Tum Var Dinha.
Extremely pleased, you granted her a boon.
Matu Putra Hit Jo Tap Kinha.
Since you performed penance for a son, O Mother.
Milahi Putra Tuhi, Buddhi Vishala.
You shall have a son with immense wisdom.
Bina Garbh Dharan Yahi Kala.
And this shall happen without conception (divinely).
Ganayak Gun Gyan Nidhana.
O leader of the Ganas, repository of virtues and knowledge.
Pujit Pratham Rup Bhagwana.
You are the first worshipped form of the Divine.
As Kahi Antardhyan Rup Hvai.
Saying this, you disappeared.
Palna Par Balak Swarup Hvai.
And you appeared as a baby in the cradle.
Bani Shishu Rudan Jabahin Tum Thana.
When you began to cry as a child.
Lakhi Mukh Sukh Nahin Gauri Samana.
Seeing your face, Gauri's happiness knew no bounds.
Sakal Magan, Sukhmangal Gavahin.
Everyone was immersed in joy, singing auspicious songs.
Nabh Te Suran Suman Varshavahin.
The gods rained flowers from the sky.
Shambhu, Uma, Bahu Daan Lutavahin.
Shiva and Uma distributed great charity.
Sur Munijan, Sut Dekhan Aavahin.
The gods and sages came to see the child.
Lakhi Ati Anand Mangal Saja.
Seeing the great joy and celebratory preparations.
Dekhan Bhi Aaye Shani Raja.
Shani Raja (Saturn) also came to see.
Nij Avgun Guni Shani Man Mahi.
Reflecting on his own curse in his mind.
Balak, Dekhan Chahat Nahi.
Shani did not want to look at the child.
Girija Kachhu Man Bhed Badhayo.
Girija (Parvati) felt a bit offended in her mind.
Utsav Mor, Na Shani Tuhi Bhayo.
It is my festival, and you do not seem to like it.
Kahan Lage Shani, Man Sakuchai.
Shani began to say, hesitating in his mind.
Ka Karihau, Shishu Mohi Dikhai.
What will happen if you show me the child?
Nahin Vishwas Uma Ur Bhayau.
Uma did not believe him.
Shani So Balak Dekhan Kahayau.
She insisted that Shani look at the child.
Padtahti Shani Drig Kon Prakasha.
As soon as Shani cast his side-glance.
Balak Sir Udi Gayo Akasha.
The child's head flew away into the sky.
Girija Giri Vikal Hvai Dharani.
Girija fell to the ground, distraught.
So Duhkh Dasha Gayo Nahin Varani.
That state of sorrow cannot be described.
Hahakar Machyau Kailasha.
A cry of despair erupted on Mount Kailash.
Shani Kinhon Lakhi Sut Ko Nasha.
Seeing the destruction of the son caused by Shani.
Turat Garuda Chadhi Vishnu Sidhaye.
Vishnu immediately rode on Garuda and went.
Kati Chakra Son Gaj Shir Laye.
He cut off an elephant's head with his discus and brought it.
Balak Ke Dhad Upar Dharayo.
He placed it upon the child's torso.
Pran Mantra Padhi Shankar Darayo.
Shankar recited the life-giving mantra and revived him.
Naam 'Ganesh' Shambhu Tab Kinhe.
Shambhu then named him 'Ganesh'.
Pratham Pujya Buddhi Nidhi Var Dinhe.
He gave him the boon of being the first worshipped and a treasure of wisdom.
Buddhi Pariksha Jab Shiv Kinha.
When Shiva tested his wisdom.
Prithvi Kar Pradakshina Linha.
He asked them to circumnavigate the earth.
Chale Shadanan Bharami Bhulai.
Shadanan (Kartikeya) went off, misguided.
Rache Baith Tum Buddhi Upai.
You sat and devised a wise solution.
Charan Matu-Pitu Ke Dhar Linhen.
You stood before your Mother and Father.
Tinke Saat Pradakshina Kinhen.
And performed seven circumnavigations around them.
Dhani Ganesh Kahi Shiv Hiya Harshe.
Praising "Blessed Ganesh", Shiva rejoiced in his heart.
Nabh Te Suran Suman Bahu Barse.
The gods rained many flowers from the sky.
Tumhari Mahima Buddhi Badai.
Your glory and the greatness of your wisdom.
Shesh Sahas Mukh Sake Na Gai.
Even the thousand-headed Sheshnaga cannot sing it fully.
Main Mati Hin Malin Dukhari.
I am devoid of wisdom, impure, and sorrowful.
Karahun Kaun Vidhi Vinay Tumhari.
How can I properly offer my prayers to you?
Bhajat 'Ramsundar' Prabhudasa.
Ramsundar is your humble servant.
Jag Prayag, Kakara Durvasa.
The world is like Prayag, and I am like Durvasa (a seeker).
Ab Prabhu Daya Din Par Kijai.
Now, O Lord, show mercy upon this poor soul.
Apni Bhakti Shakti Kuch Dijai.
Grant me some of your devotion and strength.

॥ Doha ॥
Shri Ganesh Yeh Chalisa, Path Karai Dhari Dhyan.
Whoever recites this Shri Ganesh Chalisa with full attention.
Nit Nav Mangal Mod Lahi, Milai Jagat Sanman.
Always attains joy and auspiciousness, and receives respect in the world.
Sambandh Apna Sahasra Dash, Rishi Panchami Dinesh.
Completed on the fifth day of the bright fortnight of the month of Bhadrapada (Rishi Panchami).
Puran Chalisa Bhayo, Mangal Murti Ganesh.
The Chalisa of the auspicious idol Ganesh is now complete.

faq.a1: It is a powerful devotional hymn consisting of 40 verses dedicated to Lord Ganesha, the "Vighnaharta" (remover of obstacles).
faq.q2: What are the main benefits of reciting it?
faq.a2: Reciting the Ganesh Chalisa helps remove obstacles from life, brings success in work, and provides mental peace.
faq.q3: What is the best time to recite the Ganesh Chalisa?
faq.a3: Wednesdays are considered the most auspicious day for Ganesha worship and reciting the Chalisa.
faq.q4: Does it help in improving intelligence and wisdom?
faq.a4: Yes, since Lord Ganesha is the deity of intellect and wisdom, reciting his Chalisa promotes clarity of mind and growth in knowledge.
faq.q5: Should it be read before starting a new project?
faq.a5: Absolutely, Lord Ganesha is the "Pratham Pujya" (first worshipped); reciting it before any new beginning is considered highly fruitful.
faq.q6: Who can recite the Ganesh Chalisa?
faq.a6: Anyone with a pure heart and sincere faith in Lord Ganesha can recite the Chalisa.
faq.q7: How many times should one recite it?
faq.a7: It depends on your devotion; you may recite it 1, 3, 5, or 11 times as per your convenience and time.
faq.a8: Fasting is not mandatory, but maintaining a satvik (pure) lifestyle and diet on Wednesdays is considered highly beneficial.
faq.q9: Is listening to it as beneficial as reciting it?
faq.a9: Yes, if you are unable to recite it yourself, listening to the Ganesh Chalisa with full concentration and devotion is equally meritorious.
faq.q10: Can it help in removing negative energy?
faq.a10: Yes, the recitation of the Ganesh Chalisa creates a positive aura, cleanses the environment of negativity, and brings peace to the mind.
